message:
Add theme system with initial title style

Introduce Theme struct providing centralized style definitions for
consistent UI appearance across Ratatui widgets, starting with title
style for widget blocks.

Theme design philosophy:
Rather than hardcoding ratatui::style::Style and Color values throughout
widget rendering code (creating maintenance burden when adjusting color
scheme), centralize all style definitions in Theme struct with semantic
method names (title(), highlight(), agent_color(), etc.). This enables:
- Single source of truth for color palette (dark vs light theme
  variants defined in one file)
- Easy theme switching via config (future: load_theme(config.general.theme)
  returns Theme with appropriate Color constants)
- Consistent visual language (all widget titles use Theme::title() so
  changing title color updates entire UI uniformly)

Theme::title() implementation:
Returns Style configured for widget block titles (e.g., "coding-agent-
search (press q to quit)" in current TUI placeholder, future: "Search",
"Results", "Conversation Detail" for three-pane layout):
- Foreground color: Cyan (bright, high contrast against dark terminal
  background, readable but not overwhelming like pure white)
- Modifier: BOLD for emphasis (helps titles stand out from content text,
  improves scanability when multiple panes on screen)

Style composition pattern:
Ratatui styles are composable via builder methods:
  Style::default()             // Start with no formatting
    .fg(Color::Cyan)           // Set foreground color
    .add_modifier(Modifier::BOLD)  // Add bold without removing other mods
Widgets receive Style via methods like Block::default().border_style()
or List::new().highlight_style(), compose with theme via
  .border_style(Theme::title())

Missing theme methods (planned per PLAN § 6.4):
- highlight() -> Style: Selected item in results list (reverse video +
  bold, or distinct background color for focus indication)
- agent_color(slug: &str) -> Color: Per-agent color coding (Codex: cyan,
  Claude: purple, Gemini: blue, Amp: magenta, Cline: green, OpenCode:
  yellow per PLAN § 6.2 spec), used for result list prefixes and detail
  pane headers
- status_dim() -> Style: Bottom status bar hints ("Enter:open | /:search
  | q:quit") with Modifier::DIM for subtle appearance
- border_style() -> Style: Default border color for Block widgets,
  currently defaults to terminal foreground (white/gray) but could be
  themed (e.g., dark gray borders in dark theme, light gray in light
  theme)
- background/foreground base colors: For future custom theme support
  where user provides hex codes in config.toml ("background: #1e1e1e",
  "foreground: #d4d4d4" VS Code Dark+ style)

Dark vs light theme variants (future):
Currently hardcoded for dark theme (Cyan foreground assumes dark
background). When implementing load_theme(config), Theme struct will
become:
```rust
pub struct Theme {
  title_style: Style,
  highlight_style: Style,
  agent_colors: HashMap<String, Color>,
  // ...
}
impl Theme {
  pub fn dark() -> Self { /* Cyan title, etc. */ }
  pub fn light() -> Self { /* Blue title on white, adjusted agents */ }
  pub fn title(&self) -> Style { self.title_style }
}
```
Config-driven construction allows user to select theme in config.toml:
  [general]
  theme = "dark"  # or "light"
Config module loads theme, passes to TUI via app_state, widgets render
with Theme::dark().title() or Theme::light().title().

Color palette considerations for terminal compatibility:
Using ANSI 16-color palette (Color::Cyan, Color::Blue, etc.) rather
than RGB (Color::Rgb(r, g, b)) for broader terminal compatibility:
- Works in 8-color terminals (basic xterm, Linux console) with graceful
  fallback (Cyan -> bright blue if 16-color unsupported)
- Respects user's terminal color scheme customization (Cyan maps to
  user-configured cyan in terminal preferences, not hardcoded #00FFFF)
- Lower bandwidth over SSH/serial (ANSI codes 1-2 bytes, RGB codes 14+
  bytes per color)
Future premium features (semantic search, AI summarization) could use
RGB for data visualization (e.g., heatmap of conversation activity)
where precise color control needed.

Integration with widget rendering:
Currently unused (placeholder TUI doesn't apply styles). Future usage
in three-pane layout:
```rust
terminal.draw(|f| {
  let block = Block::default()
    .title("Search")
    .borders(Borders::ALL)
    .border_style(Theme::title());  // Apply cyan + bold
  f.render_widget(block, search_area);
  // ...
})?;
```

Testing theme visually:
After implementing real TUI widgets (future commit), verify theme by:
- Launch TUI (cargo run -- tui)
- Check titles are cyan + bold, not default terminal color
- Verify readability on both dark (black/gray) and light (white)
  terminal backgrounds
- Test with 8-color terminal (TERM=xterm) to ensure fallback works

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 6.4 (theme
system), § 6.2 (color spec per agent), ui/tui.rs (terminal rendering),
Cargo.toml (ratatui dependency with style module).
